explanatory_strength.rationale: The reconciliation sharpened the mechanism and overlap controls, but did not close the decisive factual gaps.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Primary anchors are present, yet decision-critical low/base/high bridges cannot be reproduced.

downside_control.rationale: A downside case exists, but missing cycle, funding, claim or probability evidence can overwhelm it.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: The stated range is too assumption-sensitive to establish a base return above alternatives.

claims.claim: 2025 total revenue was $9.891 billion and free cash flow approximately $2.1 billion before the Apellis closing.
MS product revenue fell 7.1% while rare-disease product revenue rose 8.4%; growth products increased 19%.

strongest_counter_explanation: The model may count a portfolio narrative as diversified optionality even though clinical, reimbursement, patent and financing outcomes are correlated and the closing claims are not yet known.
most_important_missing_fact: Product-level rebates, margins, sustaining cost and post-exclusivity tails do not reproduce marketed-product values.
falsifiers: Growth products fail to offset legacy erosion
Phase 3 efficacy, safety or timing misses
specialist_findings: Asset and claim values are not tied to independently reproducible realization cash flows.
